Bio:
Tomoki Ishiara is a Partner at Sidley Austin LLP, where he serves as co-managing partner at Sidley Austin Foreign Law Joint Enterprise in the firm's Tokyo office. Elected to the partnership effective January 1, 2020, he was recognized among 30 new partners for his commitment to client service and teamwork. His practice focuses on mergers and acquisitions, with particular expertise in advising foreign investors on inbound investment into Japan, including matters involving financial regulation, compliance, and investments in Japanese companies and real estate.
Mr. Ishiara also maintains a robust intellectual property practice, providing counsel on patents, trademarks, copyrights, and design patents, as well as handling IP litigation and commercial disputes. He has significant experience in the healthcare and life sciences industries, offering regulatory guidance and compliance advice to pharmaceutical and medical device companies navigating both Japanese and international regulatory frameworks. Additionally, he advises clients on data security and privacy law matters. Mr. Ishiara has been active in the intellectual property community, serving as a reporter and panelist at professional conventions and as a member of various committees examining intellectual property rights and copyright licensing in Japan.

Bio:
Peter D. Edgerton is a Partner in the Tax practice at Sidley Austin LLP, based in the firm's Chicago office. He focuses on advising a variety of businesses, including insurance companies, private equity firms, and financial services organizations, on complex federal and state tax issues. His practice encompasses mergers and acquisitions, joint ventures, restructurings, and strategic transactions, with particular emphasis on the insurance and financial services sectors.
Peter earned his J.D. with honors from the University of Chicago Law School, where he served as articles editor of The University of Chicago Law Review. Following law school, he clerked for Judge Pamela Ann Rymer of the United States Court of Appeals for the Ninth Circuit before joining Sidley Austin. He is recognized as a leading tax attorney, having been ranked in Chambers USA for Tax in Illinois and listed as a key lawyer in The Legal 500 for US Taxes: Non-Contentious. His notable representations include advising on major insurance transactions and complex corporate deals, establishing him as a trusted adviser in sophisticated tax matters.

Bio:
Sasha Hondagneu-Messner is a Privacy and Cybersecurity Associate at Sidley Austin LLP, where he focuses his practice on privacy, cybersecurity, and AI matters. His practice encompasses regulatory investigations, data breach response, and compliance counseling involving various agencies including the NYDFS, SEC, DOJ, HHS, FINRA, FTC, and state attorneys general. He regularly advises companies on security incidents ranging from business email compromises to ransomware attacks and supply chain incidents, while also providing strategic guidance on the privacy and cybersecurity aspects of multi-billion dollar transactions.
Sasha earned his J.D. from Cornell Law School in 2020, where he served on the Cornell International Law Journal, and his B.A. *magna cum laude* from the University of Southern California in 2016. He is admitted to practice in New York and holds IAPP certifications as a Certified Information Privacy Professional/U.S. (CIPP/US) and Certified Information Privacy Manager (CIPM). Sasha serves as a young lawyer representative for the ABA Antitrust Law Section's Privacy and Information Security Committee and is an active member of the International Association of Privacy Professionals.

Bio:
Amir Freund is a counsel in the IP Litigation practice at Sidley Austin LLP, based in the firm's Palo Alto office. His practice focuses on patent infringement and trade secret litigation involving high-tech hardware and software technologies, including computer networking, telecommunications devices, semiconductors, integrated circuit design, memory circuits, fabrication processes, e-commerce, and digital radio devices. He has extensive experience litigating cases in district courts, the International Trade Commission (ITC), and Inter Partes Review (IPR) proceedings before the USPTO Patent Trial and Appeal Board.
In addition to his litigation work, Amir advises clients on a range of IP-related matters, including patent portfolio management, portfolio analysis for licensing and litigation purposes, and litigation counseling in connection with corporate transactions. He earned his LL.M. in Law, Science, and Technology from Stanford Law School in 2012 and his LL.B from Haifa University in Israel in 2007. Amir is also actively involved in pro bono work related to immigration, foster youth, and identity theft matters.

Bio:
Veronica Valenzuela is the Attorney and Owner of V.Valenzuela Law Firm, PLLC, a personal injury law firm based in Austin, Texas. As a first-generation Mexican-American and bilingual attorney, she brings over 20 years of litigation experience to her practice, specializing in car accidents, truck accidents, motorcycle collisions, and other personal injury matters. Licensed by the State Bar of Texas, Veronica earned her Juris Doctor from St. Mary's University and her B.A. in Criminal Justice from the University of Texas - San Antonio.
Throughout her career, Veronica has recovered millions of dollars in settlements for personal injury clients across Texas. After initially opening her firm in 2008 and gaining diverse experience in multiple areas of law including commercial litigation, family law, and criminal defense, she later joined a high-volume personal injury firm before reopening V.Valenzuela Law Firm in 2023 with a renewed focus on quality client representation. She is an active member of the Austin Bar Association, Travis County Women Lawyers Association, and the Texas State Bar's Women and the Law Section and Hispanic Issues Section. Beyond her legal practice, Veronica is committed to community service, volunteering as a mentor for the Seedling Program for children with incarcerated parents and serving at ReStore Austin.

Title: plastic surgeon
Company Location: 4316 James Casey Street Bldg F, Austin, TX 78745, United States
Company Description:
Austin Oculofacial Plastics and now Austin Face and Body was founded by Dr. Sean Paul with the goal to provide outstanding care to the Austin area in the practice of eyelid and facial plastic surgery.
Dr. Paul grew up in south Texas. He was accepted into the highly prestigious Business Honors Program and was named Student of the Year by the Cactus Yearbook and awarded the Outstanding Business Student of the Year of Award during his time at The University of Texas at Austin.  He stayed in Texas for medical school, where he served as Class President at The University of Texas Medical School in San Antonio.  Next, he was accepted to the highly regarded Dean McGee Eye Institute for ophthalmology residency. After residency, Dr. Paul was chosen from a highly competitive field of applicants to train for two years as an American Society of Ophthalmic Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery (ASOPRS) Fellow under the mentorship of Dr. Gerald Harris, world renowned Orbital and Oculofacial surgeon and past editor of Ophthalmic Plastic Reconstructive Surgery, at the prestigious Medical College of Wisconsin in Milwaukee. He pursued elective training in cosmetic surgery of the eyelids and face in Miami, Beverly Hills, and the greater Chicago area and is thrilled to return home to Austin, Texas.
Dr. Paul is a board certified physician and surgeon by the American Board of Ophthalmology (ABO) and a candidate member of the American Society of Ophthalmic Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery (ASOPRS). Dr. Paul specializes in eyelid and facial plastic (cosmetic and reconstructive) surgery. He uses a blended approach of non-surgical treatments and surgery, including minimal incision techniques, to provide the best patient care.  Dr. Paul has authored numerous scientific articles and has been invited to speak at many national and international meetings including Lake Como, Italy and Sydney, Australia. He has returned home to Texas and founded Austin Oculofacial Plastics in Austin, Texas.

Title: plastic surgeon
Company Location: 4316 James Casey Street Bldg F, Austin, TX 78745, United States
Company Description:
Austin Oculofacial Plastics and now Austin Face and Body was founded by Dr. Sean Paul with the goal to provide outstanding care to the Austin area in the practice of eyelid and facial plastic surgery.
Dr. Paul grew up in south Texas. He was accepted into the highly prestigious Business Honors Program and was named Student of the Year by the Cactus Yearbook and awarded the Outstanding Business Student of the Year of Award during his time at The University of Texas at Austin.  He stayed in Texas for medical school, where he served as Class President at The University of Texas Medical School in San Antonio.  Next, he was accepted to the highly regarded Dean McGee Eye Institute for ophthalmology residency. After residency, Dr. Paul was chosen from a highly competitive field of applicants to train for two years as an American Society of Ophthalmic Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery (ASOPRS) Fellow under the mentorship of Dr. Gerald Harris, world renowned Orbital and Oculofacial surgeon and past editor of Ophthalmic Plastic Reconstructive Surgery, at the prestigious Medical College of Wisconsin in Milwaukee. He pursued elective training in cosmetic surgery of the eyelids and face in Miami, Beverly Hills, and the greater Chicago area and is thrilled to return home to Austin, Texas.
Dr. Paul is a board certified physician and surgeon by the American Board of Ophthalmology (ABO) and a candidate member of the American Society of Ophthalmic Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery (ASOPRS). Dr. Paul specializes in eyelid and facial plastic (cosmetic and reconstructive) surgery. He uses a blended approach of non-surgical treatments and surgery, including minimal incision techniques, to provide the best patient care.  Dr. Paul has authored numerous scientific articles and has been invited to speak at many national and international meetings including Lake Como, Italy and Sydney, Australia. He has returned home to Texas and founded Austin Oculofacial Plastics in Austin, Texas.

Title: General Counsel
Company Location: 85 Challenger Road, Ridgefield Park, NJ 07660, United States
Company Description:
About Samsung Austin Semiconductor, LLC 
Samsung Austin Semiconductor is one of the most advanced semiconductor manufacturing facilities in the world with more than 4,300 employees, occupying 180+ acres of land out of its 606+ acres in land holdings, and 2.45 million square feet of floor space.  Through successful collaborations with foundry customers and a continuous drive to develop the most cutting-edge technologies, Samsung Austin Semiconductor has become a major player in the foundry market. Samsung Austin Semiconductor technology portfolio ranges from 65nm planar transistors to the advanced 3D FinFet technology of 14nm and 28/32nm technologies. 
Samsung Austin Semiconductor serves a global customer base with broad semiconductor process technology offerings that impact everyday life including mobile, graphic, consumer, networking/high performance computing, Internet of Things, RF and automotive.  Since 1996, its parent company, Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd has invested approximately $17 billion in Samsung Austin Semiconductor’s Austin, Texas campus, making it one of the largest direct foreign investments in United States history. 
Samsung Austin Semiconductor is a premier employer who provides a great place to work, as well as advanced and upskilled training to thriving employees. Visit www.samsung.com/us/sas.

Samsung Austin Semiconductor serves a global customer base with broad semiconductor process technology offerings that impact everyday life including mobile, graphic, consumer, networking/high performance computing, Internet of Things, RF and automotive.  Since 1996, its parent company, Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd has invested approximately $17 billion in Samsung Austin Semiconductor’s Austin, Texas campus, making it one of the largest direct foreign investments in United States history.
